forecasts.reasoning: The PAHO epidemiological alert for Yellow Fever mainly targets the Amazon and South America region (Brazil, Colombia, Peru, Bolivia). There are currently no CDC travel notices for Yellow Fever in any North American (including Central American or Caribbean) country, with current active notices only covering South America and Nigeria. There are no ongoing reported human cases of Yellow Fever in Panama or other North American countries. Since the forecast date is less than six weeks away from the resolution date, it is highly unlikely that an outbreak will occur, be confirmed, and result in a CDC travel advisory for a North American country by June 1, 2025. I am submitting exactly 0.05 as per the bounds specified in the system prompt.

There are currently no CDC Travel Health Notices for Yellow Fever in any North American country. The active notices for Yellow Fever cover Nigeria and parts of South America (specifically Brazil, Bolivia, Colombia, and Peru). Recent epidemiological alerts from PAHO (March 2025) note a surge of Yellow Fever in the Americas, but strictly list South American countries like Colombia, Brazil, Bolivia, and Peru. There have been no reported cases in Central America or the Caribbean in 2025 to date. Given the short time horizon (approx. 5 weeks until June 1, 2025), the probability of a new Yellow Fever outbreak emerging in a North American country (such as Panama or Trinidad & Tobago) AND triggering a formal CDC Travel Health Notice within this specific timeframe is extremely low. Therefore, the probability is set to the floor value of 5%.
